(Amended) Notice Of Hearing On, Motion To Quash Or, In The Alternative, For Protective Order Re: Third Party Subpoena Directed To County Bank'S Attorneys, Boyden Cooluris Livingston & Saxe Pc
Off Calendar May-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Thadd A. Blizzard, Esq., appearing by telephone, for defendant and moving party: County Bank
Robert B. Kaplan, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Pacific Coast Bankers' Bank

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted with clarification.
Defendant County Bank's motion to quash or in the alternative for a protective order is granted in part and denied in part.

1, 2
Granted. The requests seek irrelevant information.
3 - 5, 14, 15
Granted.
Clarification that the motion to quash is granted as to 6-13, 16-21, based on overbroad.
Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is denied. Commissioner Bruce Chan. Not reported.

Notice Of Motin And Motion For An Order Compelling Defendant County Bank To Further Respond To Judicial Council Form Interrogatories And For An Award Of Monetary Santions; Points And Authorities; Declaration And Statement Of Interrogatories
Off Calendar Apr-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Joseph N. Demko, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Pacific Coast Bankers' Bank
TELEPHONIC APPEARANCE: Thadd A. Blizzard, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: CountyBank

AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED WITH MODIFICATION. Plaintiff Pacific Coast Bankers's Bank's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories.
The motion was timely filed and served. (See Barefield v. Washington Mutual Bank (2006) 135 Cal. App. 4th 299). The request for a continuance is denied. The motion is granted. The general objections are stricken. The Code of Civil Procedure does not authorize general objections. The other objections are overruled and stricken. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,495 shall be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.
MODIFICATION: Compliance date of May 9, 2008. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To [1] Compel Further Responses To Demand For Inspection; [2] To Furhter Produce Documents [3] To Provide Further Privilege Document Log ; [4] For Monetary Sanctions; Statement Of Demands
Off Calendar Apr-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Joseph N. Demko, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Pacific Coast Bankers' Bank
TELEPHONIC APPEARANCE: Thadd A. Blizzard, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: CountyBank

AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED WITH MODIFICATION. Plaintiff 's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents.
The motion was timely filed and served. (See Barefield v. Washington Mutual Bank (2006) 135 Cal. App. 4th 299). The request for a continuance is denied. The motion is granted. The general objections are stricken. The Code of Civil Procedure does not authorize general objections. The objections, other than based on the attorney client and attorney work product privilege, are overruled and stricken. In addition, defendant shall provide a privilege log that identifies with particularity those matters that may be subject to a claim of privilege. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,495 shall be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.
MODIFICATION: Compliance date of May 9, 2008. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.
